sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds: Battle Born Pond, Davis Creek Pond, Idlewild Ponds, Marilyn's Pond, Paradise Ponds, Rancho San Rafael Pond, Sparks Marina Pond, Verdi Pond, Virginia Lake and Wilson Common. Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours parks are open to public use.

sources.title: Battle Born Pond Angler and Fish Survey Information — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Battle Born Pond covers an area of approximately 55 acres and is a relatively shallow body of water located in Sparks, Nevada and has a maximum depth of 12 feet. Battle Born Pond is managed as a warmwater fishery with a seasonal cold-water fishery component. It contains Largemouth Bass, Channel Catfish, Sacramento Perch, Green Sunfish, Rainbow Trout, and Common Carp. Battle Born Pond can be accessed off of Wingfield Hills Rd off between Pyramid Highway and Vista Blvd. Battle Born Pond allows for boats and vessels without motors to be used. Season is open year around, hours are when the park is open to public use. Limit is 3 gamefish, no gear restrictions.

sources.title: Battle Born Pond — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Stocking Updates (selected): 1500 Channel Catfish, 05-29-2025; 21 Largemouth Bass, 12-15 in, 09-03-2024; 79 Green Sunfish, 4 in, 09-05-2024; 1022 Rainbow Trout, 9.5 in, 05-14-2024.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Battle Born Pond is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Battle Born Pond in NDOW's current regulation table or its own angler guide; the limit applies to the combined daily/possession total across all game fish species, and the angler guide notes 'no gear restrictions'.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds (incl. Battle Born Pond)
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Battle Born Pond.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

parameters.note: NDOW's commission-regulation table lists hours for Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds generically as 'hours parks are open to public use'; NDOW's own Battle Born Pond angler guide confirms 'hours are when the park is open to public use' with no fixed clock times.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County Community Fishing Ponds
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing at Battle Born Pond is allowed during the hours the park is open to the public.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours parks are open to public use

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older; no water-specific permit exists for Battle Born Pond.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older at Battle Born Pond; no license is required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: NDOW's Battle Born Pond angler guide states the pond allows boats and vessels without motors; access and parking near the pond were still being developed as of the 2026 guide.

regulations.citation: Battle Born Pond Angler and Fish Survey Information (NDOW)
regulations.humanSummary: Only non-motorized boats and vessels are permitted on Battle Born Pond; no gas or electric motors.
regulations.verbatimText: Battle Born Pond allows for boats and vessels without motors to be used
